author: jahanshah
text:
### Name of the tool

alfa

### Tool homepage

<https://github.com/biocompibens/ALFA>

### Tool description

The tool produces plots of the raw and normalized distributions of those reads among genomic categories (stop codon, 5'-UTR, CDS, intergenic, etc.) and biotypes (protein coding genes, miRNA, tRNA, etc.).

### Tool output

<https://github.com/biocompibens/ALFA/blob/master/Images/outputs_demo.improved.png>

### Log filename pattern

*No response*

### Data suitable for MultiQC plot(s)

*No response*

### Most interesting data for the General Stats table

*No response*

author: ewels
text:
Thanks [@jahanshah](https://github.com/jahanshah) ! The PNG with example plots is useful, but really that field is for the data that MultiQC will use to generate plots within the report. So when you run `python alfa.py ...` what output files do you get? We need some text files with the numbers in that can be used to replicate those plots.

Cheers,

Phil

author: jahanshah
text:

author: ewels
text:
Great! Please can you attach it as a file? (Zip it if necessary for the file extension). Pasting to GitHub messes with whitespace which is often important for parsing.

Is there a pattern to the file names used or is it totally different according to the person running the tool? (eg. Always `counts_alpha.txt` versus `--output myfile.txt`)

author: jahanshah
text:
To my knowledge, the count files produced as the intermediate results follow the following pattern.  
`${sample_name}.ALFA_feature_counts.tsv`  
Please find attached an example of the count file and let me know if further information is required.  
Thanks again for looking after this request!  
[Samaple1.ALFA\_feature\_counts.tsv.zip](https://github.com/ewels/MultiQC/files/9419527/Samaple1.ALFA_feature_counts.tsv.zip)
